essence: Supports mechanical engineers by testing, assembling, and improving mechanical systems, components, and prototypes.

identity.title: Mechanical Engineering Technician
identity.summary: Assists engineers with mechanical design, testing, prototyping, and system optimisation across manufacturing, engineering, and industrial environments.

salary.notes: Technicians specialising in advanced testing, automation, or precision engineering can exceed £55k-£70k+.

qualifications: Training in mechanical engineering, engineering technology, or industrial maintenance
Knowledge of mechanical systems, materials, and manufacturing processes
Ability to use CAD tools, measurement equipment, and testing rigs
Understanding of engineering drawings, tolerances, and safety standards

lifestyle.description: Mechanical engineering technicians work in engineering firms, manufacturing plants, research labs, and industrial facilities. They support engineers with testing, prototyping, troubleshooting, and improving mechanical systems. The role suits individuals who enjoy hands‑on engineering, precision work, and supporting technical innovation.

habits: Assists engineers with design and analysis
Documents test results and technical findings
